title: Stock Market Today: Apple Earnings Power S&P 500, Nasdaq To Records; Trump Hits EU
author: Piero Cingari
description: U.S. equities reached all-time highs on Friday as Apple's blowout Q2 earnings powered a broad technology rally. The S&P 500 advanced 0.7% to 7,262, while the Nasdaq 100 rose 1.1% to 27,743. Apple surged 5.1% on strong revenue and guidance, while software stocks like Atlassian, Twilio, and Reddit soared on earnings beats. Energy stocks declined as crude oil fell over 3%. Some mega-cap earnings disappointed, including Amgen and Roblox, which posted significant declines.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Surged 5.1% after delivering $111.2B in Q2 revenue (up 17%), beating EPS consensus, with Greater China sales up 28% and strong forward guidance of 14-17% revenue growth
Exploded 24.4% higher on best day ever after fiscal Q3 EPS of $1.75 trounced $0.98 consensus and full-year revenue growth guidance was lifted to 24%

title: Healthcare REIT Posts 16.4% NOI Growth as Shares Soar 93%: Why This Fund's New Stake Stands Out
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: Neo Ivy Capital Management initiated a new position in American Healthcare REIT by purchasing 136,925 shares worth approximately $6.44 million. The REIT delivered strong 16.4% same-store NOI growth in Q3, driven by senior housing and integrated senior health campuses segments. Shares have surged 93% over the past year, with management raising full-year guidance.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong 16.4% same-store NOI growth, 93% one-year stock appreciation, raised full-year NFFO guidance, solid GAAP net income of $55.9 million, and sustained occupancy gains above 90% in senior housing demonstrate solid operational performance and positive demographic tailwinds.
Mentioned only as a top holding of Neo Ivy Capital Management with no specific performance data or analysis provided in the article.

title: Is Dolby Stock a Buy or Sell After the CEO Sold Shares Worth $2.5 Million?
author: Robert Izquierdo
description: Dolby Laboratories CEO Kevin J. Yeaman sold 36,699 shares worth approximately $2.5 million on November 24, 2025. Despite the sale, he retains significant direct and indirect ownership. The company reported mixed fiscal year results with modest revenue growth and forecasts continued growth in fiscal 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mixed financial performance with slight revenue increase, modest growth forecast, and a stock price near 52-week low. The CEO's share sale appears routine and does not indicate significant concern, as he maintains substantial share ownership.

title: Dolby Laboratories Reports Third Quarter 2025 Financial Results
author: Prnewswire
description: Dolby Laboratories reported strong Q3 2025 financial results with total revenue of $316 million, up from $289 million in the same quarter last year. The company saw growth across various technology platforms including Dolby Atmos and Dolby Vision in multiple industries.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Increased revenue, strong engagement across multiple sectors, positive financial outlook, and expansion of technology into new device categories
Launched first smartphone with Dolby Vision Capture
Launched smartphones with Dolby Vision and Dolby Atmos

title: Home Audio Equipment Market Size to Hit USD 89.5 Billion by 2033 | Straits Research
author: N/A
description: The global home audio equipment market is projected to grow from $33.2 billion in 2025 to $89.5 billion by 2033, driven by increasing demand for high-quality, immersive audio experiences and innovations in audio technologies.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article highlights Dolby Laboratories' role in developing advanced audio technologies, such as Dolby Atmos, which are driving growth in the home audio equipment market.
